gaps.why: You cannot optimize or debug production AI without observability. This is the #1 gap in most AI teams.
gaps.trend: LLM observability is the fastest growing AI infra category in 2025-2026.

essentialRepos.reason: Standard for LLM tracing and prompt management
Open source LLM observability and evals
OpenTelemetry-native, one line integration

gaps.description: You have 29 repos covering Observability & Monitoring.

gaps.why: Without evals, you are shipping AI blindly. Production AI teams run evals in CI on every deploy.
gaps.trend: Evals moved from research to standard engineering practice in 2025. Every serious team uses them.

essentialRepos.reason: pytest for LLMs — run in CI pipeline
RAG-specific evaluation, critical for RAG pipelines
Standard eval framework from OpenAI
Standard for benchmarking open models

gaps.description: You have 201 repos covering Evals & Benchmarking.

gaps.why: vLLM changed inference. PagedAttention and continuous batching are now table stakes for serving LLMs.
gaps.trend: Inference optimization is the hottest AI infra topic of early 2026. Token throughput is the new benchmark.

essentialRepos.reason: Fastest open source LLM inference. Industry standard.
Run any model locally. Foundation of local AI.
150k stars. Easiest way to run models locally.

gaps.description: You have 157 repos covering Inference & Serving.

gaps.why: Getting reliable structured data from LLMs is unsolved for most teams. Instructor and Outlines solve this.
gaps.trend: Structured outputs became critical in 2025 as agents need reliable JSON. Pydantic + LLMs is now standard.

essentialRepos.reason: Structured outputs using Pydantic. 9.5k stars.
Guaranteed structured generation. 11k stars.
Control LLM output structure. From Microsoft.

gaps.description: You have 48 repos covering Structured Output & Reliability.

gaps.why: Stateful agents require persistent memory. This is the core unsolved problem for production agents in 2026.
gaps.trend: Agent memory frameworks are the #1 accelerating category in commit velocity in early 2026.

essentialRepos.reason: Memory layer for AI. 26k stars and growing fast.
Stateful agents with persistent memory (MemGPT).

gaps.description: You have 69 repos covering Agent Memory & Context.

gaps.why: Generic models are not good enough for production. Fine-tuning on domain data is becoming standard practice.
gaps.trend: Unsloth made fine-tuning 2x faster with 70% less memory. Barrier to fine-tuning dropped significantly in 2025.

essentialRepos.reason: 2x faster fine-tuning, 70% less memory. 28k stars.
Production fine-tuning toolkit.
RLHF, DPO, GRPO training from HuggingFace.

gaps.description: You have 76 repos covering Model Fine-tuning.

gaps.why: Prompt injection and jailbreaks are real attack vectors. AI security is now a production concern, not just research.
gaps.trend: Enterprises are requiring AI security audits before deployment. Red teaming tools are seeing rapid adoption.

essentialRepos.reason: LLM vulnerability scanner. Standard red team tool.
Microsoft's Python Risk Identification Toolkit.

gaps.description: You have 196 repos covering Security & AI Safety.

gaps.why: Reproducibility and experiment tracking are essential for iterating on models in production.
gaps.trend: MLflow became the default open source experiment tracker. Integration with LLM workflows is now standard.

essentialRepos.reason: Experiment tracking and model registry. 19k stars.
Weights & Biases SDK. Training visualization.
Git for ML datasets. 14k stars.

gaps.description: You have 57 repos covering MLOps & Experiment Tracking.

gaps.why: AI coding agents are the fastest productivity multiplier for engineers in 2026. Essential for any AI-native team.
gaps.trend: OpenHands (50k stars), Cline (40k), and Aider (25k) are transforming how engineers work.

essentialRepos.reason: Open source Devin. 50k stars. Fastest growing.
Autonomous coding in VS Code. 40k stars.
Pair programming with LLMs. 25k stars.

gaps.description: You have 85 repos covering Coding Assistants.

gaps.why: DeepSeek-R1 and OpenAI o3 changed what reasoning models can do. Understanding this space is essential for AI PMs.
gaps.trend: Reasoning models are the defining AI story of early 2026. Open source reasoning now matches closed models.

essentialRepos.reason: Open reasoning model that shocked the industry.
Open source DeepSeek-R1 reproduction from HuggingFace.
